MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Betty Utterback/ Katherine Utterback, et al.

RULING:
CLAIMANT COUNTY OF SACRAMENTO'S MOTION TO IMPRESS LIEN AND APPLICATION FOR DISTRIBUTION OF SETTLEMENT PROCEEDS

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: County of Sacramento's motion to impress lien and application for distribution of settlement proceeds is denied. The County failed to produce evidence to show that it did not contribute to Mrs. Utterback's injury/death. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Dana Freed of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10602 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Defendant Campbell Soup Co.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-06-2011 Continued To Apr-26-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 220
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Campbell Soup Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication as to issue 1 (negligence) are continued to April 26, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. to be heard after defendant's motion for a protective order regarding the plaintiffs' notice to depose defendant's PMK and COR and defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar as to issues 2-5 (strict liability, false representation, civil battery and punitive damages). If defendant succeeds in obtaining a protective order precluding the deposition of its PMK and COR, then the tentative ruling is to grant defendant's motion for summary judgment on the grounds that defendant satisfied its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain admissible evidence that defendant knew or should have known that its disturbance of asbestos-containing materials created a safety hazard for a member of Russell Utterback's household. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Dr. Richard Cohen are sustained. If defendant is not successful in obtaining a protective order precluding the deposition of its PMK and COR, then the tentative ruling is to grant plaintiffs' request for a continuance per CCP 437c(h) to a date after the completion of the deposition of defendant's PMK and/or COR. The foregoing tentative rulings are based on the assumption but not a formal decision that California law recognizes a cause of action sounding in negligence against a premises owner by a family member of an employee of an independent contractor of the premises owner based on 'take-home' exposure to asbestos-containing dust. Plaintiffs dismissed their claims for strict liability, false representation, civil battery and punitive damages. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION To Appoint Katherine Utterback As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Pltf And For Leave To File 1st Amended Complt
Off Calendar Jan-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gibert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Betty Utterback
No other appearances.

RULING:
Plaintiff Betty Utterback's motion to appoint Katherine Utterback as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for, deceased plaintiff and for leave to file first amended complaint. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion is granted. Plaintiff has satisfied all procedural requirements under CCP 377.32 to establish Katherine Utterback as successor in interest for purposes of bring a survival action. Ms. Utterback signed a declaration pursuant to 377.32 under penalty of perjury which states that she is authorized to act as the successor-in-interest. The cases cited by Defendant Monterey Mechanical are distinguishable. All involved contests as to who was the rightful successor-in-interest amongst surviving heirs. Here, no heirs or beneficiaries have contested Ms. Utterback's appointment. Probate code 8468 strongly suggests that it is not necessary for the next of kin to be appointed as decedent's representative unless he or she claims a right of appointment. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Brenda Fields, CSR # 7235
